channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Election Commission extends SIR schedule for multiple states and Delhi
item.description: The Election Commission of India has revised the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) schedule for Karnataka, Telangana, Punjab, and Delhi, extending it by 10-12 days. This adjustment affects the timeline...

item.title: First Lady's Support Helped Akara Vendor Expand Business
item.description: Ayo Alakara, a Nigerian akara vendor and UNN graduate, shared how financial support from First Lady Oluremi Tinubu helped him scale his business. He expanded from selling under a tree to owning a food...

item.title: Bali’s Ancient Rice Terraces: How a 1,000-Year-Old Water System Faced Vanishing and Water Scarcity
item.description: Bali's iconic rice terraces, which once formed an intricate ancient water management system spanning over a millennium, are now facing severe threats. Recent reports reveal that approximately 6,500 he...

item.title: Canadian Securities Regulators Update Cybersecurity Guidance After Reviewing Firms' Practices
item.description: Canada’s securities regulators have updated their cybersecurity guidance following a review of 73 registered firms, identifying gaps in their practices. The Canadian Securities Administrators (CSA), w...

item.title: Khampepe commission continues inquiry into apartheid-era crimes
item.description: The Khampepe commission of inquiry, tasked with investigating alleged attempts to obstruct the prosecution of apartheid-era perpetrators, is holding its proceedings in Johannesburg. The commission, es...

item.title: Alas Pilipinas Men Fall to Cambodia in 5 Sets During SEA V Cup
item.description: The Alas Pilipinas men's volleyball team suffered a 3-2 loss to Cambodia in the SEA V Cup, marking a significant setback in their campaign. The match, held on July 15, 2026, was a crucial encounter as...

item.title: Anambra Legislators Call for Immediate Evacuation of Stranded Anambra Citizens in South Africa
item.description: The Anambra State House of Assembly has called on Governor Chukwuma Soludo and philanthropists to intervene in the plight of Anambra indigenes stranded in South Africa, urging their evacuation and saf...

item.title: Tanzanian Court Approves Legal Challenge Against Police Chief's Dismissal Decision
item.description: A former police officer in Tanzania has been granted court permission to challenge the decision made by the Inspector General of Police, marking a significant development in employment disputes within...

item.title: Liverpool police investigate domestic abuse complaint involving man and woman
item.description: Liverpool police have arrested a man following a complaint from a woman alleging domestic abuse. The incident occurred in the city's Merseyside area, where the woman reportedly accused her partner of ...

item.title: Bangkok Bar Fire Claims 32 Lives; Government Vows Safety Overhaul
item.description: A devastating fire at Bangkok's Rong Beer Na Lad Phrao restaurant and bar on Sunday night resulted in 32 deaths and over 70 injuries, prompting Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul to announce a nationw...

item.title: Sunderland signs experienced defender as Bournemouth targets young talent
item.description: Sunderland FC has completed the signing of Belgium international Thomas Meunier on a two-year contract. The 34-year-old full-back joins Wearside after leaving French club Lille, becoming the Black Cat...

item.title: FSCA investigates PIC governance amid CEO suspension and whistleblower claims
item.description: The Financial Sector Conduct Authority (FSCA) has initiated an investigation into the Public Investment Corporation (PIC) following the suspension of its CEO, Patrick Dlamini, as a precautionary measu...

item.title: Simba SC confirms Anicet Oura's continued commitment for 2026/2027 season
item.description: Tanzanian football giants Simba SC have officially denied rumors suggesting Ivorian attacking midfielder Anicet Oura is set to leave the club. Team information officer Ahmed Ally clarified that no for...

item.title: Sandra Mbuvi's Bold Black Outfit Sparks Mixed Reactions at Janet Kaingi's Wedding
item.description: Sandra Mbuvi, daughter of former Nairobi Governor Mike Sonko, became one of the most talked-about guests during the white wedding of her adopted sister, Janet Kaingi, and Elvis Kintu at Limuru Gardens...

item.title: Albanese government announces new AI office and proposes standards requiring large data centres to match their electricity use with power generation
item.description: The Albanese government has announced a significant shift in Australia's approach to artificial intelligence by introducing a new regulatory framework aimed at supporting the country's ambitions to be...

item.title: Enid Bennett: A Pioneering Actress of Early Hollywood
item.description: Enid Bennett (1893-1969) was an Australian-born silent film actress who became a prominent figure in early American cinema during the late 1910s and early 1920s. After arriving in the U.S. in 1915, sh...

item.title: FIFA's Brand Value Surpasses $5.2 Billion Following 2026 World Cup Success
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, hosted jointly by the United States, Canada, and Mexico, has significantly boosted the organization's brand value, according to a Brand Finance Africa analysis. The tournament...

item.title: E Atkinson to Miss Second Test Against New Zealand Following Curfew Breach and ECB Probe into Nightclub Incident
item.description: The article details the potential exclusion of England cricketer E Atkinson from the second Test against New Zealand due to a curfew violation. The ECB has initiated an investigation into a nightclub ...

item.title: Southern California Faces Elevated Wildfire Risk Amid Extreme Heatwave
item.description: A severe heatwave is expected to peak in Southern California on Wednesday, pushing temperatures into triple-digit territory and heightening wildfire risks. Meteorologist Mike Wofford of the National W...

item.title: Canadian Forces and Police Safely Remove Historic Explosive Device from Portapique Shore
item.description: Canadian police and military authorities have successfully removed an old military ordnance that washed ashore in Portapique, Nova Scotia. The explosive device, discovered in the water near Beachcombe...
